title: Genesis: Isaac and Covenant Line
objective: Deepen biblical-theological understanding of genesis: isaac and covenant line and its place in redemptive history.

required_passages.text: Selected key passages for this unit segment, read in full during lesson delivery.

segments.audio_script: Today we study genesis: isaac and covenant line and trace God's covenant faithfulness.
Listen to the assigned passage and note covenant themes, divine actions, and human response.
We place this text in its historical setting, canonical location, and literary flow.
We analyze theological meaning, covenant development, and connections to the wider biblical story.
Key themes include God's holiness, covenant faithfulness, judgment and mercy, and redemptive purpose.
Review question: What was the name given to Jacob after he wrestled with God, and what does that name mean? Answer: Israel, meaning one who strives with God or God strives.
segments.correct_answer: Israel, meaning one who strives with God
acceptable_variants: Israel — he who struggles with God
jacob was renamed israel after the wrestling

segments.audio_script: Carry this lesson into prayer, worship, and careful Scripture reading as we continue the course.

vocabulary.definition: A visible appearance of God to a human being; Jacob's wrestling in Genesis 32 is one of the most striking theophanies in the Old Testament
The eldest son's inheritance rights, including a double portion; Esau sold his birthright to Jacob for a meal in Genesis 25
